text:
CT CHEST, ABDOMEN AND PELVIS WITH CONTRAST

INDICATION: 59-year-old female with history of FIGO Stage IIIC high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma, BRCA1 germline mutation positive, status post primary debulking surgery with optimal cytoreduction, followed by adjuvant carboplatin/paclitaxel x6 cycles (completed approximately 9 months prior). Currently on niraparib maintenance therapy (approximately 6 months). Surveillance restaging imaging. Prior CT after cycle 3 of chemotherapy demonstrated complete radiographic response. CA-125 at time of scan: 21 U/mL.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following the administration of 100 mL of Omnipaque 350 intravenous contrast. Acquisitions obtained in the portal venous phase. Sagittal and coronal reformats generated and reviewed. Comparison made to prior CT dated [PRIOR STUDY DATE].

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The lungs are clear. No focal consolidation, ground-glass opacity, or pleural-based nodularity. The airways are patent to the subsegmental level bilaterally. No endobronchial lesion identified.

Pleura: No pleural effusion. No pneumothorax. No pleural thickening or nodularity.

Mediastinum and Hila: The heart is normal in size. No pericardial effusion. The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber without aneurysmal dilatation. Mediastinal and hilar contours are unremarkable. No pathologically enlarged lymph nodes in the mediastinum, hila, or axillae by size criteria. A few sub-centimeter mediastinal nodes are present and unchanged, felt to be reactive or incidental.

Chest Wall and Osseous Structures: No destructive bony lesion. Mild multilevel degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.

---

ABDOMEN:

Liver: The liver is normal in size and demonstrates homogeneous enhancement. No focal hepatic lesion identified. The intrahepatic bile ducts are not dilated.

Gallbladder/Biliary: The gallbladder is surgically absent, consistent with prior cholecystectomy. Post-cholecystectomy changes are noted at the gallbladder fossa with a small amount of stable fatty replacement. No biliary ductal dilatation. Common bile duct measures 5 mm, within normal limits for post-cholecystectomy status.

Spleen: The spleen is surgically absent. Splenectomy changes are noted in the left upper quadrant with expected soft tissue stranding and volume loss in the splenic bed. No new mass or fluid collection in this region.

Pancreas: The pancreas enhances homogeneously. No pancreatic ductal dilatation. No peripancreatic fluid or mass.

Adrenal Glands: Both adrenal glands appear normal. No adrenal nodule or thickening.

Kidneys and Ureters: Both kidneys are normal in size and demonstrate symmetric nephrographic enhancement. No hydronephrosis. No renal calculi identified. No perinephric stranding.

Stomach and Bowel: Stomach is decompressed and normal in appearance. Small bowel loops are normal in caliber and distribution. No bowel wall thickening. No evidence of obstruction. The colon appears unremarkable throughout. No pneumoperitoneum.

Omentum and Peritoneum: The omentum is surgically resected; omentectomy defect is noted with expected post-operative changes. No omental caking or infiltration. The peritoneal surfaces, mesentery, and paracolic gutters are smooth and clean in appearance. No peritoneal nodularity. No ascites identified. The subphrenic spaces are clear bilaterally.

Abdominal Lymph Nodes: No enlarged retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymph nodes. Several small para-aortic and aortocaval nodes measuring up to 7 mm in short axis are present and unchanged from the prior examination — these remain below threshold for size-based concern and are not suspicious.

Abdominal Wall and Vasculature: The abdominal aorta is normal in caliber. No significant atherosclerotic calcification. Infrarenal aorta and iliac vessels patent. The portal and superior mesenteric veins are patent. No abdominal wall hernia.

---

PELVIS:

Post-Surgical Changes: Extensive post-surgical changes are noted in the pelvis consistent with the patient's history of bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy and pelvic peritoneal debulking. There is expected volume loss and fibrous stranding in the adnexal regions bilaterally, unchanged from prior. A small focus of pelvic adhesive change along the right pelvic sidewall is noted and appears unchanged. No pelvic fluid collection or abscess.

Uterus: The uterus is absent (hysterectomy changes confirmed). The vaginal cuff appears intact without thickening or nodularity.

Bladder: The urinary bladder is partially distended and demonstrates no wall thickening, intraluminal filling defect, or perivesical infiltration.

Rectum and Sigmoid: The rectum and sigmoid colon are unremarkable. No perirectal or presacral soft tissue mass.

Pelvic Lymph Nodes: No pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy. The iliac chains are clear.

Osseous Structures: The visualized pelvis and lumbar spine demonstrate mild degenerative changes. No suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesion.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. No evidence of disease recurrence. The lungs, liver, peritoneum, omentectomy bed, and pelvic operative field are clear. No ascites, no adenopathy, no new soft tissue lesion.

2. Post-surgical changes are stable and expected, including absent spleen (splenectomy), absent gallbladder (cholecystectomy), omentectomy bed changes, and bilateral adnexal/pelvic debulking changes. No complications.

3. Sub-centimeter para-aortic lymph nodes and mediastinal nodes are unchanged and remain below threshold for concern.

4. Overall, the findings are consistent with continued complete radiographic response in the setting of ongoing niraparib maintenance therapy.

Dictated and electronically signed by: [Radiologist Name], MD
Department of Radiology
[Institution Name]
